falsification_rule_text: Not supported if, across the five labelled cases: (a) the p10 wage FIRST-ORDER effect is positive and significant (confirming the acknowledged success) AND (b) at least 3 of 5 cases show no statistically meaningful teen-E/P divergence (>1.0pp below synthetic control at p<0.10) AND (c) no case shows the NEET third-order persistence. If FIRST-ORDER is confirmed but SECOND-ORDER and THIRD-ORDER effects are null across the majority of cases, the causal-chain claim is refuted even though the first-order mechanism is intact. The minimum-wage-employment literature places prior at a moderate level: bite ratio is the decisive moderator.

variables_loaded.name: regional_gdp_growth
variables_loaded.source: world_bank_wdi:NY.GDP.MKTP.KD (national-level proxy when sub-national series unavailable)

variables_missing.name: teen_employment_to_population_ratio_high_bite
variables_missing.source: bls:LAU (US state teen E/P); ons:LFS (UK regional teen E/P); destatis:mikrozensus (DEU regional youth E/P)

variables_missing.name: low_skill_immigrant_employment_rate
variables_missing.source: bls:CPS (US foreign-born by education); ons:APS (UK); destatis:mikrozensus (DEU foreign-born by education)

variables_missing.name: neet_rate_15_24
variables_missing.source: oecd:NEET (15-24 not in employment, education, or training, by region)

variables_missing.name: informal_sector_employment_share
variables_missing.source: ilo:ILOSTAT; proxies: US 1099/contractor share from BLS-CES, UK self-employment share from ONS

variables_missing.name: wage_at_10th_percentile
variables_missing.source: bls:OEWS (US state p10 hourly wage); ons:ASHE (UK regional p10); destatis:VSE (DEU Kreis p10)

variables_missing.name: minimum_wage_bite_ratio
variables_missing.source: constructed: statutory minimum / median wage in same region and age-group; inputs from bls:OEWS, ons:ASHE, destatis:VSE
